instruction: Count how many emails in Inbox contain the keyword "code" in the email body (search across all pages), then save JSON to "$HOME/Desktop/mail_body_keyword_count_3_1.json". Return JSON only (no extra text). Required keys: keyword (string), count (integer). Do not include extra keys.
reference_command: all=$(for p in {1..50}; do out=$(himalaya envelope list --folder Inbox --page "$p" --page-size 100 -o json body "code" 2>/dev/null || true); [ -z "$out" ] && break; [ "$out" = "[]" ] && break; echo "$out"; done); if [ -z "$all" ]; then count=0; else count=$(printf '%s\n' "$all" | jq -s 'add | length'); fi; jq -n --arg keyword "code" --argjson count "$count" '{keyword:$keyword,count:$count}' > "$HOME/Desktop/mail_body_keyword_count_3_1.json"

parameters.expected_command: all=$(for p in {1..50}; do out=$(himalaya envelope list --folder Inbox --page "$p" --page-size 100 -o json body "code" 2>/dev/null || true); [ -z "$out" ] && break; [ "$out" = "[]" ] && break; echo "$out"; done); if [ -z "$all" ]; then count=0; else count=$(printf '%s\n' "$all" | jq -s 'add | length'); fi; jq -n --arg keyword "code" --argjson count "$count" '{keyword:$keyword,count:$count}'
